items.title: Fever score the seventh-most points by any team ever and blast Connecticut 123-88. Highest field goal percentage by the Fever in a single game (61.6%). Their three stars combined for 72 points. A dominant outing for win number 17. Fever enter the break 17-10.

- Record 70pts in a half
- Record 123pts
- Led by as many as 35
- Clark with 27/11a, 16th time with at least 20/10
- Mitchell (23pts) with 20+ pts and 3+ 3s in 11 straight games.

Next: All-Star Weekend

I am grateful for being a part of the best broadcast crew in the NBA and capturing the rise of the team. 

I wish nothing but the best for the terrific staff and Rip City fans. ✌️

Brett retired in 1993.

items.title: The Sky are 9-18, but the season has been defined as much by how they've lost as by how often.
items.body_excerpt:
The Sky are 9-18, but the season has been defined as much by how they've lost as by how often.

Thirteen of their 18 losses have come by 10 points or fewer. 

Despite sitting 13th in the WNBA standings, they own the league's eighth-best point differential (-2.2).

items.title: Asked Lynne Roberts last night why the live ball turnovers have been such a problem for the Sparks lately: “If I knew, I’d fix it. I don’t know. … Those are the ones that just, you know, they had 20 points off our turnovers. That’s the game.”
items.body_excerpt: Asked Lynne Roberts last night why the live ball turnovers have been such a problem for the Sparks lately: “If I knew, I’d fix it. I don’t know. … Those are the ones that just, you know, they had 20 points off our turnovers. That’s the game.”

items.title: Asked Dearica Hamby how the Sparks can use this All-Star break to get refreshed: “We showed last year we were able to really respond after the All-Star break and having a complete, healthy team. Looking forward to getting [Kelsey Plum] back and the second half of basketball.”
items.body_excerpt: Asked Dearica Hamby how the Sparks can use this All-Star break to get refreshed: “We showed last year we were able to really respond after the All-Star break and having a complete, healthy team. Looking forward to getting [Kelsey Plum] back and the second half of basketball.”

items.title: Asked Lynne Roberts if she feels there’s added pressure on the Sparks to figure it out post-ASB: “I don’t think there was ever not pressure to figure it out. It doesn’t change. … In terms of pressure, this is what we do. ... I want to succeed, and we will.”
items.body_excerpt: Asked Lynne Roberts if she feels there’s added pressure on the Sparks to figure it out post-ASB: “I don’t think there was ever not pressure to figure it out. It doesn’t change. … In terms of pressure, this is what we do. ... I want to succeed, and we will.”

items.title: A well-deserved 3-year, $15M extension for Jordan Walsh.
items.body_excerpt:
A well-deserved 3-year, $15M extension for Jordan Walsh.

Last season, he showcased his versatility and effectiveness while guarding players ranging from Maxey to KAT.

Now, he wants to pair his growth as a shooter & impact on the offensive glass with a new identity on that end.

Story ⬇️

 -DC: Nicknamed "Coast"
-Gilliam: "Jay Vincent...scored 43 on him"
-Gervin: "He'd grab you+ say...stand still" 
-Kiki: "Couldn't guard his own shadow"
-Nique: "could guard...but didn't want to very often"

items.title: The Odyssey - it was enjoyable. Felt a like a string of cameos but the action moved. Take a pair of ear plugs. In my old age, it’s been awesome to shave off 20-30 dbs of loud movies.

items.title: One of my favorite parts of Summer League is seeing everyone in one place. I talk with at least one person from every team, plus agents. The result: an overflowing notebook!
items.body_excerpt:
One of my favorite parts of Summer League is seeing everyone in one place. I talk with at least one person from every team, plus agents. The result: an overflowing notebook!

Here is the East, plus notes on the second apron, cap growth and more!

www.spotrac.com/news/_/id/34...

media.title: 2026 NBA Eastern Conference Summer League Notebook | Spotrac News
media.description: Keith Smith spent 11 days in Las Vegas gathering exclusive, insider insights on the major financial trends and roster moves defining this season for every Eastern Conference team.

items.title: I emptied out half of my notebook from Las Vegas here. Notes and quotes from all 15 Eastern Conference teams. Plus some thoughts on the second apron, the lack of rookie scale extensions and lessened cap growth from those in and around the NBA.
items.body_excerpt:
I emptied out half of my notebook from Las Vegas here. Notes and quotes from all 15 Eastern Conference teams. Plus some thoughts on the second apron, the lack of rookie scale extensions and lessened cap growth from those in and around the NBA.

Check it out. Western Conference notes coming soon!

items.title: How first round picks performed at NBA summer league with stats ordered left to right by how meaningfully they translate to the regular season. Give more credence to the stats on the left side and mostly ignore the right side.
items.body_excerpt:
How first round picks performed at NBA summer league with stats ordered left to right by how meaningfully they translate to the regular season. Give more credence to the stats on the left side and mostly ignore the right side.

Heat Map Key:

   Lighter = better
   Darker = worse

images.alt:
Table titled "2026 NBA Draft Class Summer League Stats | First Round," subtitled "Summer League stats ordered from left to right from most to least predictive of rookie season performance." A color-coded heatmap shows 29 first-round picks (rows) across 21 stat columns, grouped into three bands: MORE MEANINGFUL (3PAR, 3PA/36, AST/36, BLK/36, REB/36, OREB/36), LESS MEANINGFUL (DREB/36, PF/36, FGA/36, TOV/36, FTR, AST/TO, FTA/36, PT/36, STL/36), and NOT MEANINGFUL (FT%, EFG%, TS%, MPG, 3P%, +/- per 36). R² values run from 0.70 for 3PAR down to 0.02 for +/- per 36. Cells are shaded dark purple for low values through magenta and orange to yellow for high values.

Selected rows — pick, player, team, minutes: 1 AJ Dybantsa (WAS) 50.7; 2 Darryn Peterson (UTA) 139.6; 3 Cameron Boozer (MEM) 194.1; 4 Caleb Wilson (CHI) 110.7; 5 Keaton Wagler (LAC) 112.8; 6 Mikel Brown Jr. (BKN) 86.6; 7 Darius Acuff Jr. (SAC) 140.9; 8 Kingston Flemings (ATL) 102.9; 9 Morez Johnson Jr. (DAL) 60.6; 10 Brayden Burries (MIL) 124.5; 11 Yaxel Lendeborg (GSW) 183.2; 12 Aday Mara (OKC) 119.6; 13 Nate Ament (MIL) 117.0; 14 Hannes Steinbach (CHA) 121.6; 15 Dailyn Swain (CHI) 112.1; 16 Bennett Stirtz (OKC) 137.2; 17 Ebuka Okorie (DET) 152.2; 18 Christian Anderson (CHA) 134.2; 19 Allen Graves (TOR) 109.9; 22 Labaron Philon (PHI) 127.8; 23 Zuby Ejiofor (ATL) 103.9; 24 Cameron Carr (LAL) 177.0; 25 Sergio de Larrea (DAL) 113.4; 26 Tarris Reed Jr. (SAS) 121.4; 27 Chris Cenac Jr. (BOS) 93.3; 28 Joshua Jefferson (BKN) 63.6; 29 Alex Karaban (SAC) 132.2; 30 Koa Peat (PHX) 108.8.

Notable values: Mikel Brown Jr. leads 3PAR at 0.53 alongside Christian Anderson and Sergio de Larrea at 0.61; Kingston Flemings leads AST/36 at 9.4 and Sergio de Larrea at 11.1; Hannes Steinbach leads REB/36 at 13.6; Aday Mara posts 10.5 REB/36 with 2.7 BLK/36; Zuby Ejiofor posts 12.5 REB/36 and 6.2 OREB/36. Footer: "Stat predictiveness via research by Owen Phillips | Player data: nbadraft.app | Viz: @HoopInformatics.bsky.social."

items.title: I thought today was Wednesday all day, then accepted two hours ago that it was actually Friday, and only just now am I realizing it's been Thursday the entire time. The summer is a weird time.

items.title: That scene where Odysseus sits on the steps and talks about the shame of the sack of Troy was on par for me with Chingachgook’s revenge scene in Last of the Mohicans and the run by Schofield in 1917. Just a perfect crescendo of raw feelings and music.
items.body_excerpt: That scene where Odysseus sits on the steps and talks about the shame of the sack of Troy was on par for me with Chingachgook’s revenge scene in Last of the Mohicans and the run by Schofield in 1917. Just a perfect crescendo of raw feelings and music.

items.title: The Invite is one of the best surprises of the year. I know everyone (myself included) is still wrapped up in The Odyssey, but if you have the opportunity see this one in theaters, it’s an excellent, funny, poignant, witty movie that deserves your support:
